FactoryTalk® Services Platform FTSP - Weak Authentication via JWT Validation Bypass
Severity:
High
Advisory ID:
SD1786
Published Date:
July 14, 2026
Last Updated:
July 14, 2026
Revision Number:
1.0

No

Yes
Workaround:
No
CVE IDs

Downloads
The following link(s) provide the security advisory in
Vulnerability Exploitability Exchange
format:
JSON
Summary
The security of our products is important to us as your industrial automation supplier. This security issue was found internally during routine testing and is being reported based on our commitment to customer transparency and improvement of all business environments.
Product Description
FactoryTalk® Services Platform facilitates communication between FactoryTalk® components, enabling data exchange and interaction across computers in a FactoryTalk® Directory.
Affected Products and Solution
Affected Product
CVE
Affected Software Version
Corrected in Software Version
Affected Catalog Numbers
FactoryTalk - FactoryTalk® Directory (FTSP)
CVE-2026-10714
6.60
6.60 (see more information in Mitigations and Workarounds section)
RAID 1158263
N/A
Security Issue Details for CVE-2026-10714
Category
Details
CVE ID
CVE-2026-10714
Impact
A security issue exists within FactoryTalk® Services Platform (FTSP), allowing an attacker to bypass JWT signature validation during Okta Web Authentication. The vulnerability stems from the application not verifying that the JWT algorithm is configured for RSA, enabling an attacker to set the algorithm to "none" and craft forged tokens. This could allow an authenticated low-privilege user to impersonate any authorized user on the FTSP server, resulting in unauthorized access to system configuration and the ability to grant permissions to other systems protected by FTSP.
CVSS 3.1 Base Score
7.8/10
CVSS 4.0 Base Score
8.8/10
CWE
CWE-1390: Weak Authentication
Known Exploited Vulnerability
No
Mitigations and Workarounds
Customers using FactoryTalk® Services Platform v6.60 should apply either the individual patch (RAID 1158263) or the February 2026 Patch Roll-up, or later update.
Customers using the affected software, who are not able to upgrade to one of the corrected versions, should use our
security best practices
.
Revision History
Revision
Date
Description
1.0
7/14/2026
Initial release
Glossary:
·
Authentication:
The process of verifying the identity of a user or system.
·
JWT:
(JSON Web Token) A compact, URL-safe token format used to securely transmit claims between two parties, typically consisting of a header, payload, and cryptographic signature used for authentication and authorization.
·
RSA:
(Rivest–Shamir–Adleman) A widely used asymmetric cryptographic algorithm that uses a public-private key pair to securely sign and verify data, commonly employed in digital signatures and token validation.
